sglang.title: [Feature] Support DeepEP Low Latency
sglang.author: liz-badada
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Support DeepEP low latency dispatch / combine, introduce a new command-line argument --deepep-mode to specify DeepEP mode (auto, normal and low_latency). Additionally, we believe DeepEP is particularly well-suited for PD disaggregation. Also in low-latency mode, the CUDA Graph feature fu

sglang.title: update bench_serving
sglang.author: zhyncs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Running Unit Tests.
 Update documentation / docstrings / example tutorials as needed, according to Writing Documentation.
 Provide throughput / latency benchma

sglang.title: Prevent memory leak of retract_decode when page_size > 1
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Prior PRs (#4914, #4913) partially fix the corner case bug of retract_decode when page_size > 1, but there is still memory leak risk when prefix_indices is not page aligned, which can happen with cache_unfinished_req.

Modifications
Changing padding to truncating to correctly release the

vllm.title: [V1] Implement sliding window attention in kv_cache_manager
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Build on top of #14079, should be merged after it.
This pr supports “real” sliding window in v1:

Support dropping blocks outside sliding window
For prefix caching, only requires the last sliding_window tokens to be cached to achieve a prefix cache hit. e.g., for request ABCDE with sliding window si

vllm.title: fix: can not use uv run collect_env close #13888
vllm.author: yihong0618
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #13888
This patch close #13888 by using uv pip when there's no pip in the
uv venv env.
For uv run there will be an env name UV so we can use it.

vllm.title: [Feature] specify model in config.yaml
vllm.author: wayzeng
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix #14855
Run CLI:

vllm serve SOME_MODEL: correct.
vllm serve SOME_MODEL --config config.yaml: correct. model_tag in CLI, takes precedence over model in config file.
vllm serve --config config.yaml: correct if model is in config
If missing model in CLI and config file, will raise error.
If --model

vllm.title: [Misc] Fix speculative config repr string
vllm.author: ShangmingCai
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Minor fix since using prompt_lookup_max to identify whether "ngram" is used has been deprecated, and we have the method param to indicate which speculative method is used currently, it should be in the repr string.

vllm.title: [Docs] Fix small error in link text
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Changes "The Transformers enables..." -> "The Transformers backend enables..."

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix no video/image profiling edge case for `MultiModalDataParser`
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #15802 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)

Given limit_mm_per_prompt={'image': 1, 'video': 0}, passthrough_data will be {'video_embeds': []}, which cause incorrect prompt match.
This PR avoids parsing empty embeds data for parse_mm_data.

vllm.title: [Misc] Use envs.VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_CHANNEL_TYPE
vllm.author: ruisearch42
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Use envs.VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_CHANNEL_TYPE instead of envs.VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_NCCL_CHANNEL.
Since ray 2.42 , the behavior of using with_tensor_transport(transport='auto') is that Compiled Graph will use NCCL if both the sender and receiver are using GPU (instead of CPU), which is almost

vllm.title: setup correct nvcc version with CUDA_HOME
vllm.author: chenyang78
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, our cmake config does not set CMAKE_CUDA_COMPILER with respect to the CUDA_HOME setup. For example, before this fix, with the command below:
CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-12.4 pip install -e

we still got the following:
//CUDA compiler
CMAKE_CUDA_COMPILER:FILEPATH=/usr/local/cuda/bin/nvcc

Th

vllm.title: [Model] Support Mistral3 in the HF Transformers format
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Works for text input and single image batches. Requires a fix to the pixtral processing in Transformers (huggingface/transformers#37019). It still fails to succeed on a full eval of chartqa in vLLM V1, seemingly due to batching encoding issues, so I forced this model to only run with V0 for now.

vllm.title: Add option to use DeepGemm contiguous grouped gemm kernel for fused MoE operations.
vllm.author: bnellnm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add the option to use DeepGemm's grouped gemm kernel for fused_moe operations.
Benchmark setup
python3 benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_moe.py --dtype=fp8_w8a8 -tp ${TP} --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 --trust-remote-code --use-deep-gemm
Note: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 has similar problem sizes
E=256
N=40

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Clean up LoRA tests
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
To reduce the testing time for LoRA and ensure  coverage of all LoRA features in testing, the following modifications were made:

Remove  long context lora  layer tests
Only test certain models on V1, such as Baichuan
Remove models that fail testing on V1, such as Gemma
Temporarily skip some redunda

CLOSES #14216
Introduction
This PR introduces support for the Aya Vision models by CohereForAI. Aya Vision models excel in multilingual and multimodal tasks, significantly advancing performance in vision-language understanding.
Supported models:

Aya Vision 32B
Aya Vision 8B

vllm.author: ZZBoom
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR is intended to support the MiniMaxText01 model inference.
It can run on a single machine with 8xH800 and 8xH20, where a single H800 machine can handle a maximum context input of 2 million tokens, and a single H20 machine can handle a maximum context input of 5 million tokens.

vllm.title: [V1] Fix: make sure `k_index` is int64 for `apply_top_k_only`
vllm.author: b8zhong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The sampler in vLLM fails with
RuntimeError: gather(): Expected dtype int64 for index
because the index tensor for torch.gather isn’t cast to torch.int64, resulting in a dtype mismatch error. this cast should be safe.

Bug reproduction
python benchmarks/benchmark_serving.py \
    --backend deepspeed

vllm.author: WoosukKwon

Changes:

Speculative metrics --> SpecDecoding metrics
Pint the acceptance rate in %
Remove the trailing ,
Shorten the log

BEFORE:

AFTER:
SpecDecoding metrics: Draft acceptance rate: 100.

vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes the typo in #15834 that breaks two-node 4GPU test on CI since method object is always evaluated to True in Python when it's not being called.
Caught this bug from CI errors since we don't have TPU on CI but it's calling forward_tpu
